intro:
Unfortunately, I could not find any information on the plant "Cynoglossospermum cymosum." However, based on the prefix "Cynoglossospermum" which is derived from "cynoglossum" (a genus of flowering plants commonly known as hounds-tongue) and "spermum," I assume this plant might be a member of the same genus or related to it. 

If we consider it to be related to Cynoglossum officinale, here's a paragraph about it:

Cynoglossum officinale, or Cynoglossum cymosum as per your query, is a perennial herb belonging to the Boraginaceae family, commonly known as hounds-tongue. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum officinale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cynoglossum officinale is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used in capsules or powder form for its therapeutic benefits.
